Our People

The Hands Behind
the Mission

A passionate team rooted in Sirsa, Haryana, working together to create meaningful change.

Hiral
Co-Founder

Passionate about youth empowerment and grassroots impact. Led the Stationery Donation Drive with six dedicated volunteers, bringing smiles to 50 children at Bhai Kanhaiya Ashram.

What We Stand For

Our Core Values

Every action we take is guided by these principles.

🕊️
Peace

We believe that lasting change begins with peaceful coexistence. Every initiative we undertake promotes dialogue, understanding, and reconciliation within communities.

🤝
Unity

Together we are stronger. Hands2Hold brings together volunteers, donors, and community members — united by a single purpose: to lift those who need it most.

🌸
Harmony

We work in harmony with local communities, respecting their culture, needs, and aspirations. Our support is rooted in listening before acting.

Our Work

Projects & Initiatives

Two completed drives, countless lives touched — every project is a step forward.

Completed
Stationery Donation Drive
📍 Bhai Kanhaiya Ashram, Sirsa 📅 30th May 2025

Thousands of children from economically disadvantaged backgrounds lack access to basic school supplies — a barrier to learning that is unacceptable in the world's fourth largest economy. Hands2Hold came together and donated 50 packets of stationery to Bhai Kanhaiya Ashram, Sirsa. Co-founder Hiral, along with six dedicated volunteers, distributed these kits to children aged 5–16 from economically disadvantaged and specially-abled backgrounds.

"Fifty stationery kits may seem like a small number, but for fifty children, they represented fifty new opportunities to learn, create, and dream."
Completed
Period Care Drive
📍 Slum Area, Sirsa 📅 12th June 2025

Menstrual hygiene remains a taboo and a barrier for millions of women and girls. Hands2Hold took action by distributing pads, diapers, and atta to families living in the slum areas of Sirsa. The drive reached 100+ people and directly supported 22 families — providing not just essential supplies, but dignity and care to those who need it most.

"Breaking the silence, one family at a time — because every woman deserves to live with dignity."
